LAVA

Overview

LAVA (Log ratio of Ancestral Variances) is an R package designed to help researchers analyze genomic data through the lens of ancestral variance ratios. The package provides tools for statistical analysis and visualization of evolutionary patterns.

Installation

You can install the development version of LAVA from GitHub with:

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("isadoo/LAVA")

Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to use LAVA:

library(LAVA)
# Basic example code will be added once package functionality is implemented
hello()
#> [1] "Hello, world!"

Features

The LAVA package provides:

  • Analysis tools for variance comparison
  • Utility functions for data preprocessing
  • Visualization tools for results interpretation

Documentation

For more detailed information, check out the LAVA website.

Contributing

Contributions to LAVA are welcome! Please see our contributing guide to get started.

License

LAVA is licensed under the GPL-3 license. See the LICENSE file for more details.
